releases.author: pitfa19
releases.description:
**Anatomed — 3D Anatomy for Obsidian · v0.1.1**

Addresses the Obsidian community-review findings:

- No dynamic script-element creation in the shipped bundle (neutralized react-dom's dead resource-hoisting path at build time).
- No filesystem access — dropped the unused `node:fs`/`node:path` disk loaders from the browser build (the catalogue is bundled; neighbour data is fetched at runtime).
- Dropped the unsupported `ui-sans-serif` CSS keyword from the widget font stack.

**Install manually:** download `manifest.json`, `main.js`, and `styles.css` into `<your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/anatomed/`, then enable in **Settings → Community plugins**. Desktop only.

releases.author: pitfa19
releases.description:
**Anatomed — 3D Anatomy for Obsidian · v0.1.0** (first release)

Embed interactive, region-isolated 3D anatomy inline in your notes. Write an `anatomed` code block and get a rotatable 3D model showing only the structures you asked for, with a toggleable legend, hover-to-name, and click-to-open `[[Structure]]` notes.

**Install manually:** download `manifest.json`, `main.js`, and `styles.css` from this release into `<your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/anatomed/`, then enable the plugin in **Settings → Community plugins**. Desktop only.

**Licensing:** software under MIT; the 3D anatomy models and derived data under CC BY-SA 4.0, derived from [Z-Anatomy](https://www.z-anatomy.com/) (Kervyn & Zielinski) → BodyParts3D (DBCLS). See `LICENSE`, `LICENSE-ASSETS`, `NOTICE`.
